66. 
67. TRD-023 — Graceful Degradation for Partial Teams (AC: FR-GD-4, FR-GD-5, FR-GD-6, AC-TD-4):
68. Case 1: REVIEWER_ENABLED=false (no reviewer role defined):
69. - Skip review step entirely
70. - After builder: validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_qa') if QA_ENABLED else validate_transition(bead_id, 'closed')
71. - br comment add <bead_id> 'status:skip-review lead:<agent> reason:no-reviewer-role-defined'
72. Case 2: QA_ENABLED=false (no qa role defined):
73. - Skip QA step entirely
74. - After reviewer approval: validate_transition(bead_id, 'closed')
75. - br comment add <bead_id> 'status:skip-qa lead:<agent> reason:no-qa-role-defined'
76. Case 3: REVIEWER_ENABLED=false AND QA_ENABLED=false:
77. - Lead orchestration loop active, builder implements, lead closes directly
78. - After builder: validate_transition(bead_id, 'closed')
79. - br comment add <bead_id> 'status:closed lead:<agent> reason:no-reviewer-no-qa-direct-close'
80. Case 4: Only lead+builder defined (same as Case 3):
81. - Identical to Case 3
82. Record which steps are skipped in the team configuration summary printed during this Preflight step.

### Step 10: Marketplace Preflight Check

Before execution begins, check for marketplace capability gaps that may affect team
config quality. Presents suggestions for missing agents/skills and installs approved
plugins. Re-reads team config from TRD after installation if agents referenced in TRD
team config are now available. AC: FR-11.1 through FR-11.6, AC-8.1 through AC-8.6

**Actions:**
1. Step 1 — Load marketplace.json: Read marketplace.json from repository root
2. If missing or malformed: log 'marketplace.json not found -- skipping marketplace preflight'; skip remaining steps
3. Step 2 — Domain scan: identify TRD task domains using same keyword matching as /create-trd Phase 4 Step 3
4. Step 3 — Installed-plugin detection: for each plugin in marketplace.json derive local path from source field; use Glob to check packages/<name>/ directory; build PREFLIGHT_INSTALLED set
5. Step 4 — Gap analysis: for each TRD domain check if default agent exists in packages/*/agents/*.yaml registry; for framework-specific keywords check corresponding skills/ directory
6. Exclude plugins already in PREFLIGHT_INSTALLED; consolidate multiple gaps pointing to same plugin
7. Context-aware filtering: generic 'test' keyword alone must NOT trigger testing framework suggestions
8. Step 5 — Track declined plugins within this session: build SESSION_DECLINED_PLUGINS set (empty at start)
9. Step 6 — For each gap suggestion (excluding SESSION_DECLINED_PLUGINS):
10. If ask_user is available: present yes/no prompt with plugin name, description, rationale, agents/skills provided
11. If user approves: run 'claude plugin install <plugin_name>' via Bash; verify packages/<name>/ directory created
12. If user declines: add to SESSION_DECLINED_PLUGINS (do not re-suggest during this session)
13. If non-interactive: log suggestion as [INFO] and skip all installations
14. Step 7 — If any plugins were installed:
15. Re-run agent discovery: scan packages/*/agents/*.yaml to refresh KNOWN_AGENTS and AGENT_REGISTRY
16. If TEAM_CONFIG_SOURCE='trd': re-read ## Team Configuration from TRD and re-validate agents against refreshed registry
17. If newly installed agents ARE referenced in TRD team config: log 'Newly installed agents now available for team config'
18. If newly installed agents are NOT referenced in TRD team config: log 'Note: newly installed agents not referenced in TRD team config. Consider re-running /create-trd to update team configuration.'
19. Step 8 — If user declines all suggestions: proceed with existing team config and available agents

### Step 11: Traceability Validation Gate

Run validate-requirements as an automatic preflight gate before scaffolding begins.
Checks that PRD requirements have TRD task coverage, that [satisfies] annotations
reference real REQ-NNN IDs, and that every user-facing task has a paired -TEST task.
Errors (orphaned annotations) halt execution; warnings are printed but do not halt.
Skipped if TRD has no [satisfies] annotations (legacy TRD without traceability).

**Actions:**
1. Step 1 — Check for traceability annotations: scan TRD content for any '[satisfies REQ-' token
2. If NO [satisfies REQ-] annotations found: print 'NOTE: TRD has no [satisfies] annotations — skipping traceability validation (legacy TRD). Run /create-trd to regenerate with traceability.'; skip remaining steps
3. Step 2 — Locate PRD reference: search TRD for PRD path using priority order: (1) docs/PRD/*.md link (most specific), (2) 'Based on PRD: <path>', (3) 'PRD: <path>'. After extracting a candidate path, verify it looks like a file path (contains '/' or ends in '.md') before setting PRD_PATH.
4. If PRD path found: set PRD_PATH to that path
5. If PRD path NOT found: print 'WARNING: No PRD reference found in TRD — skipping traceability validation. Add a PRD reference to enable this gate.'; skip remaining steps
6. Verify PRD_PATH exists on disk. If not: print 'WARNING: PRD file not found at <PRD_PATH> — skipping traceability validation. Fix the PRD path in the TRD.'; skip remaining validation steps and proceed to Scaffold.
7. Step 3 — Run validation: invoke the validate-requirements logic inline (do not spawn a new agent):
8. a. Parse PRD_PATH for all REQ-NNN IDs and AC-NNN-M IDs — build PRD_REQUIREMENTS map
9. If PRD_REQUIREMENTS is empty AND PRD file is non-empty: print 'WARNING: PRD parsed but no REQ-NNN headings found — PRD may use a different format. Skipping validation.' Skip remaining validation steps and proceed to Scaffold.
10. b. Parse TRD for all [satisfies REQ-NNN], [verifies TRD-NNN], and Validates PRD ACs: fields — build TRD_TASKS map
11. If TRD_TASKS is empty AND TRD is non-empty: print 'WARNING: TRD parsed but no [satisfies] annotations found — TRD may use a different format. Skipping validation.' Skip remaining validation steps and proceed to Scaffold.
12. c. Check coverage: for each PRD REQ-NNN, check if any TRD task has [satisfies REQ-NNN]; collect UNCOVERED as WARNING
13. d. Check orphans: for each [satisfies REQ-NNN] in TRD, check if REQ-NNN exists in PRD; collect ORPHANED as ERROR
14. e. Check test pairs: for each impl task with [satisfies REQ-NNN] (not INFRA/ARCH), check if <task-id>-TEST exists; collect MISSING_TESTS as WARNING
15. f. AC reference check: for each task in TRD with 'Validates PRD ACs:' field, check if each AC-NNN-M exists in PRD_REQUIREMENTS (i.e., in the flat set of AC-NNN-M IDs extracted from the PRD); collect INVALID_AC_REFS as WARNING
16. Step 4 — Print validation report:
17. Print '=== TRACEABILITY VALIDATION ==='
18. Print each WARNING (uncovered reqs, missing -TEST pairs)
19. Print each ERROR (orphaned annotations)
20. Print '=============================='
21. Step 5 — HALT decision: if any ERRORS (orphaned annotations) found:
22. Print 'ERROR: Traceability validation failed. Fix orphaned [satisfies] annotations in TRD before implementing.'
23. Print 'Run /ensemble:validate-requirements <prd-path> <trd-path> for details.'
24. HALT
25. Note: No beads were created. No git operations were performed. Plugin installations from this session are permanent. Re-run after fixing the TRD annotations.
26. Step 6 — If only WARNINGs (no errors): print 'Traceability warnings found but continuing. Address before closing implementation. Run /ensemble:validate-requirements <PRD_PATH> <TRD_PATH> at any time to review warnings.' and proceed to Scaffold

## Phase 2: Scaffold

### Step 1: TRD Parsing

Parse TRD into structured phases and tasks

**Actions:**
1. Pass 1: Extract TRD_TITLE from first H1 heading
2. Pass 2: Extract TRD_SUMMARY from first prose paragraph (max 500 chars)
3. Pass 3: Extract phases from '### Phase N' or '### Sprint N' headings; synthesize single phase if none found
4. Pass 4: Extract tasks matching '- [ ] **TRD-XXX**: Description' pattern; assign to phases by proximity
5. Pass 5: Validate at least one task found; warn on duplicate task IDs
6. Pass 6: Extract traceability annotations per task — look for [satisfies REQ-NNN] (may also be [satisfies INFRA] or [satisfies ARCH]), [verifies TRD-NNN], 'Validates PRD ACs: AC-NNN-M,...', 'Implementation AC:' block, 'Proof of requirement:' field; store in TASK_TRACEABILITY map keyed by task.id
7. Pass 7: Classify task type — if task.id ends in '-TEST' suffix, mark is_test_task=true; extract verifies_task_id (from [verifies TRD-NNN]) and satisfies_req_id (from [satisfies REQ-NNN]); store in TASK_TRACEABILITY[task.id]

### Step 2: Idempotency Cache

Cache existing beads to enable partial scaffold resume via title-prefix matching

**Actions:**
1. Run: br list --status=open --json (capture full JSON output once)
2. Parse JSON array of bead objects with .id and .title fields
3. Build EXISTING_BEADS map by matching title prefixes: [trd:<TRD_SLUG>] for epic, [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:phase:<N>] for stories, [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:<ID>] for tasks
4. Map key is the title prefix pattern, value is the bead .id
5. Use this cache for all 'already exists' checks during scaffold — do not re-query per bead

### Step 3: Root Epic Creation

Create the top-level epic bead for the TRD

**Actions:**
1. Check EXISTING_BEADS for title prefix [trd:<TRD_SLUG>]
2. If found: ROOT_EPIC_ID = existing id; skip creation
3. If not found: run br create --title='[trd:<TRD_SLUG>] Implement TRD: <TRD_TITLE>' --type=epic --priority=2 --description='<TRD_SUMMARY>' --json
4. Capture ROOT_EPIC_ID by parsing .id field from JSON response
5. HALT if exit code != 0 or ROOT_EPIC_ID empty

### Step 4: Story Bead Creation

Create one story bead per TRD phase under the root epic

**Actions:**
1. For each phase i: check EXISTING_BEADS for title prefix [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:phase:<i>]
2. If found: STORY_BEAD_IDs[i] = existing id; skip creation
3. If not found: run br create --title='[trd:<TRD_SLUG>:phase:<i>] Phase <i>: <phase.title>' --type=feature --priority=2 --description='Phase <i> of TRD: <TRD_TITLE>. Contains <task_count> tasks.' --json
4. Capture STORY_BEAD_ID by parsing .id from JSON response
5. After creation: br dep add <ROOT_EPIC_ID> <STORY_BEAD_ID> to establish story-blocks-epic relationship (epic cannot close until all stories close)
6. HALT if any creation fails

### Step 5: Task Bead Creation

Create one task bead per TRD task under its phase story with full description from TRD actions

**Actions:**
1. For each task: check EXISTING_BEADS for title prefix [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:<task.id>]
2. If found: TASK_BEAD_IDs[i][j] = existing id; record in TRD_TO_BEAD_MAP; skip creation
3. If not found: build structured bead description based on task classification from TASK_TRACEABILITY:
4. For impl tasks (is_test_task=false): build description as: '## Task: <task.id>\nTRD Reference: <TRD_FILE_PATH>#<task.id-lowercase>\nPRD Reference: <PRD_FILE_PATH>#<satisfies_req_id-lowercase> (omit this line if satisfies_req_id is INFRA or ARCH)\nSatisfies: <satisfies_req_id or INFRA/ARCH>\nPRD ACs: <validates_acs>\nTarget File: <file>\nActions:\n<numbered_actions>\nImplementation AC:\n<implementation_ac_checklist>\nDependencies: <depends_on>'
5. For test tasks (is_test_task=true): build description as: '## Test Task: <task.id>\nTRD Reference: <TRD_FILE_PATH>#<task.id-lowercase>\nPRD Reference: <PRD_FILE_PATH>#<satisfies_req_id-lowercase>\nVerifies Task: <TRD_FILE_PATH>#<verifies_task_id-lowercase>\nVerifies: <verifies_task_id>\nSatisfies: <satisfies_req_id>\nPRD ACs Proven: <validates_acs>\nProof of requirement: <proof_text>\nTarget Files: <files>\nActions:\n<numbered_actions>\nTest AC:\n<test_ac_checklist>\nDependencies: <depends_on>'
6. Fallback (task has no [satisfies] annotation AND no [verifies] annotation): use raw TRD task body as description. Tasks with [satisfies INFRA] or [satisfies ARCH] use the impl task format.
7. Run: br create --title='[trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:<task.id>] <task.description>' --type=task --priority=<task.priority> --description='<structured_bead_description>' --json
8. The description should include: target file path, numbered action items, dependencies, satisfaction/verification links, and acceptance criteria from the TRD task entry
9. Capture TASK_BEAD_ID by parsing .id from JSON response
10. After creation: br dep add <STORY_BEAD_ID> <TASK_BEAD_ID> to establish task-blocks-story relationship (story cannot close until all its tasks close)
11. Record TRD_TO_BEAD_MAP[task.id] = bead_id for each task

### Step 6: Dependency Encoding

Wire explicit TRD dependencies and inter-phase sequential gates

**Actions:**
1. For each task with depends_on: br dep add <TASK_BEAD_ID> <TRD_TO_BEAD_MAP[dep_id]> (warn and skip if dep not in map)
2. For each phase i >= 2: br dep add <first_task_of_phase_i> <last_task_of_phase_i-1> (inter-phase sequential gate)

### Step 7: BV Execution Planning

Run bv robot-plan and robot-triage for graph-aware execution planning

**Actions:**
1. Run: br sync --flush-only (ensure JSONL is current before any bv call)
2. If BV_AVAILABLE == true:
3. Run: PLAN_OUTPUT=$(bv --robot-plan --format toon) — capture parallel execution tracks
4. Parse PLAN_OUTPUT to extract parallel tracks (track numbers, task lists per track)
5. Store PARALLEL_TRACKS for use in wheel instructions
6. On bv failure: echo 'WARNING: bv --robot-plan failed. Falling back to sequential execution.'; BV_AVAILABLE=false
7. Run: TRIAGE_OUTPUT=$(bv --robot-triage --format toon) — capture triage analysis
8. Parse TRIAGE_OUTPUT to extract: quick_ref, recommendations (ranked list with scores), quick_wins, blockers_to_clear
9. Store TRIAGE_RECOMMENDATIONS for use in wheel instructions
10. On bv failure: echo 'WARNING: bv --robot-triage failed.'; continue without triage data
11. If BV_AVAILABLE == false: skip bv calls, use br-only sequential execution order

### Step 8: Scaffold Summary and BV Analysis

Print scaffolding summary with BV analysis output

**Actions:**
1. Print scaffolding summary: epic ID, story count, task count, dep count
2. Run: br list --status=open for summary overview
3. Run: br sync --flush-only (final sync after all scaffold mutations)
4. If BV_AVAILABLE == true:
5. Print section: === BV ANALYSIS ===
6. Print PARALLEL EXECUTION TRACKS with parsed track data from PLAN_OUTPUT
7. Print TRIAGE RECOMMENDATIONS with top recommendations from TRIAGE_OUTPUT
8. Print QUICK WINS from TRIAGE_OUTPUT quick_wins section
9. Print BLOCKERS TO CLEAR from TRIAGE_OUTPUT blockers_to_clear section
10. If BV_AVAILABLE == false: print 'BV analysis unavailable. Using br-only execution order.'

### Step 9: Foreman Wheel Instructions Output

Print Foreman-based execution instructions for multi-agent parallel implementation. AC: FR-WI-1, FR-WI-2, FR-WI-3, FR-WI-4, AC-WI-1, AC-WI-2

**Actions:**
1. Print the following Foreman execution instructions:
2. ================================================================
3. FOREMAN EXECUTION INSTRUCTIONS
4. ================================================================
5. Foreman orchestrates parallel agents across isolated git worktrees.
6. Each bead runs through: Explorer → Developer → QA → Reviewer → Finalize
7. ----------------------------------------------------------------
8. PREREQUISITES (run once):
9. foreman doctor                    # Verify br, API key, dependencies
10. foreman init --name '<TRD_SLUG>'  # Initialize Foreman (if not done)
11. ----------------------------------------------------------------
12. RUN (dispatches agents to all ready beads):
13. foreman run --max-agents <max_parallel>
14. # Foreman reads br ready, assigns beads to agents, runs pipeline,
15. # merges completed branches, and closes beads automatically.
16. ----------------------------------------------------------------
17. MONITOR:
18. foreman status --watch            # Live agent + bead status
19. foreman inbox --all --watch        # Live inter-agent messages
20. ----------------------------------------------------------------
21. DEBUG:
22. foreman debug <bead-id>           # AI analysis of failing bead
23. foreman inbox --bead <bead-id>    # Full message timeline for bead
24. ----------------------------------------------------------------
25. MERGE (runs automatically in foreman run loop):
26. foreman merge --target-branch feature/<TRD_SLUG>
27. ================================================================
28. If BV_AVAILABLE == true: also print the BV analysis from Scaffold Step 7 (parallel tracks and triage recommendations) above the Foreman instructions as planning context.
29. If PLAN_ONLY=true: print 'Plan complete. Bead hierarchy created. Run /ensemble:implement-trd-beads --execute (or foreman run) to begin implementation.' and EXIT. Do not enter Execute phase.
30. TIP: You can also run /ensemble:beads-plan <ROOT_EPIC_ID> at any time to regenerate bv analysis and Foreman instructions without re-running TRD scaffold.

## Phase 3: Execute

### Step 1: Execution Loop

Poll bv robot-next (or br ready) and execute tasks until epic is complete. AC: FR-GD-1, FR-GD-2, FR-GD-3, AC-TD-3, AC-BC-1

**Actions:**
1. NOTE: This Execute phase implements the same execution engine as /ensemble:beads-build. If you have raw beads (not from a TRD), use /ensemble:beads-build <epic-id> instead. beads-build also accepts --trd <path> to enable TRD augmentations on any bead hierarchy.
2. Resume check: If TASK_TRACEABILITY is empty (cross-session resume — Scaffold phase did not run this session), re-parse TRD content to rebuild TASK_TRACEABILITY before processing tasks:
3. a. Re-run Scaffold Step 1 Pass 6: extract [satisfies REQ-NNN], [satisfies INFRA], [satisfies ARCH], [verifies TRD-NNN], 'Validates PRD ACs:' fields, 'Implementation AC:' blocks, and 'Proof of requirement:' fields per task
4. b. Re-run Scaffold Step 1 Pass 7: classify task type — if task.id ends in '-TEST' suffix mark is_test_task=true; extract verifies_task_id and satisfies_req_id
5. c. Store rebuilt map in TASK_TRACEABILITY keyed by task.id
6. d. Print 'NOTE: TASK_TRACEABILITY rebuilt from TRD (cross-session resume). Tasks: <N>'
7. e. After rebuild: if rebuilt TASK_TRACEABILITY is empty AND TRD content was successfully read AND TRD is non-empty: print 'WARNING: TRD re-parse found no traceability annotations. Requirement audit comments will not be written. If this is a legacy TRD without [satisfies] annotations, this is expected.' If TRD file could not be read: print 'ERROR: Cannot read TRD file at <TRD_PATH> during cross-session resume. Verify file exists and is readable.' and HALT.
8. Context Budget Monitoring (applies to both TEAM_MODE=true and TEAM_MODE=false):
9. After every 5 task completions OR after any task that produces more than 500 lines of output:
10. Print context budget warning: 'Context checkpoint: <N> tasks completed this session. If quality is degrading, consider:'
11. '  1. /compact to compress conversation context'
12. '  2. Start a new session with /ensemble:implement-trd-beads --execute <trd-path> (beads preserve all state)'
13. '  3. Use max parallel N to delegate more work to fresh-context subagents'
14. This is informational only — do not halt or pause execution
15. 
16. TEAM_MODE Gate (evaluated once at the start of the Execute phase):
17. if TEAM_MODE == false:
18. - Use the existing v2.1.0 Execute loop (all steps 1-6 unchanged)
19. - Skip all team-specific steps (reviewer delegation, QA delegation, rejection loop, parallel builders)
20. - Quality Gate phase: run full scope (current behavior)
21. - Continue to LOOP below
22. if TEAM_MODE == true:
23. - Replace the standard execution loop with the Lead Orchestration Loop below (TRD-013, AC: FR-LL-1, AC-LL-1)
24. - Reviewer delegation: enabled (TRD-016)
25. - QA delegation: enabled if QA_ENABLED=true (TRD-017)
26. - Parallel builder slots: active (TRD-025)
27. - Quality Gate phase: reduced scope if QA_ENABLED=true (TRD-031)
28. - Note: Scaffold phase is IDENTICAL in both modes (no team awareness in scaffold)
29. - Note: Completion phase is IDENTICAL in both modes
30. - Note: v2.1.0 beads without status: comments are treated as in_progress (builder stage) — safe default for backward compat (TRD-038)
31. - RETURN (team loop handles all remaining execution; skip LOOP below)
32. 
33. LEAD ORCHESTRATION LOOP (TEAM_MODE=true):
34. 
35. Variables: active_builders = {} (bead_id -> builder_agent)
36. 
37. LOOP:
38. 1. br sync --flush-only (ensure JSONL current)
39. 
40. 2. Check in-flight tasks (tasks with br native status=in_progress that have sub-states in_review or in_qa):
41. - Run: br list --status=in_progress --json, filter by TRD slug
42. - For each in-progress bead: call get_sub_state(bead_id)
43. - If sub_state == 'in_review': proceed to Reviewer Delegation step (TRD-016)
44. - If sub_state == 'in_qa': proceed to QA Delegation step (TRD-017)
45. - If sub_state == 'in_progress': task is with builder (normal)
46. 
47. 3. Count available slots: available_slots = max_parallel - len(active_builders)
48. (default max_parallel=1, increased by 'max parallel N' argument)
49. 
50. 4. If available_slots > 0:
51. - Get next tasks: if EXECUTE_ONLY=true or BV_AVAILABLE, run bv --robot-next --format toon (returns top unblocked task; bv is guaranteed available when EXECUTE_ONLY=true)
52. Else (EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND not BV_AVAILABLE): run br ready, filter by [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:] prefix
53. - For each task (up to available_slots):
54. a. (TRD-021) Architecture Review: check task description for keywords:
55. 'architecture', 'design', 'system', 'cross-cutting', 'multi-component', 'orchestrat'
56. If ANY keyword found:
57. - Lead performs brief architectural review (read TRD context, consider phase impact)
58. - Generate guidance notes (key design decisions, patterns to use, pitfalls to avoid)
59. - Record: br comment add <bead_id> 'architecture-review lead:<agent> guidance:<url_encoded_summary>'
60. - Include guidance in builder prompt: 'Architecture guidance from lead: <guidance_notes>'
61. If NO keywords: skip architecture review, proceed normally
62. b. (TRD-020) Lead Skip Decision: evaluate task characteristics before delegating to builder:
63. - If task description contains ONLY documentation/comments (no code changes expected):
64. SKIP_REVIEW=true, SKIP_QA=true
65. - If REVIEWER_ENABLED=false: SKIP_REVIEW=true (per TRD-023)
66. - If QA_ENABLED=false: SKIP_QA=true (per TRD-023)
67. - Lead can also skip based on judgment (low-risk configuration, etc.)
68. Record skip decisions:
69. - If SKIP_REVIEW: br comment add <bead_id> 'status:skip-review lead:<agent> reason:<rationale>'
70. - If SKIP_QA: br comment add <bead_id> 'status:skip-qa lead:<agent> reason:<rationale>'
71. Transition directly:
72. - Both review and QA skipped: validate_transition(bead_id, 'closed') directly after builder
73. - Only review skipped: validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_qa') -> QA delegation after builder
74. - No skips: normal flow (in_progress -> in_review -> in_qa -> closed)
75. AC: FR-LL-6, FR-LL-7, FR-LL-10, AC-LL-3, AC-LL-4, AC-LL-5, AC-LL-6
76. c. (TRD-022) Sibling Task Context: collect completed sibling tasks in same phase:
77. - Query br list --status=closed --json, filter by [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:phase:<N>] prefix
78. - For each closed task in this phase: read br comment list, find last implementation summary
79. - Limit to 5 most recently closed siblings
80. - If sibling context found, add to builder prompt:
81. 'Previously completed tasks in this phase:'
82. '[TRD-XXX]: <implementation_summary_first_200_chars>'
83. '[TRD-YYY]: <implementation_summary_first_200_chars>'
84. - If no siblings completed yet: omit this section from prompt
85. AC: FR-LL-9
86. d. Select builder from TEAM_ROLES.builder.agents using keyword matching (TRD-014)
87. e. validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_progress') -- write status comment
88. f. Delegate to builder via Task(subagent_type=<builder>, prompt=<builder_prompt>) (TRD-015)
89. g. active_builders[bead_id] = builder
90. 
91. --- Parallel Builder Execution (TRD-025) ---
92. When available_slots > 1 AND multiple tasks are ready, apply parallel dispatch:
93. 
94. TRD-025-1. Set BUILDER_SLOTS = max_parallel (default 1, overridden by 'max parallel N' argument)
95. 
96. TRD-025-2. Query available tasks for parallel dispatch:
97. - If EXECUTE_ONLY=true or BV_AVAILABLE: run bv --robot-plan --format toon to get parallel execution tracks
98. Extract up to BUILDER_SLOTS tasks from the top-priority track(s)
99. - Else (EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND not BV_AVAILABLE): run br ready, filter by [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:] prefix, take up to BUILDER_SLOTS entries
100. - Candidate task list: CANDIDATE_TASKS = up to BUILDER_SLOTS ready tasks
101. 
102. TRD-025-3. File conflict detection: prevent assigning tasks with overlapping file targets
103. - For each task in CANDIDATE_TASKS:
104. a. Extract target file paths from the bead description
105. (look for 'File:', file path patterns like src/*, and file mentions in action items)
106. b. Build FILE_MAP: task_id -> [file_path, ...]
107. - Build SEEN_FILES = {} (file_path -> first task_id that claims it)
108. - Iterate CANDIDATE_TASKS in order:
109. For each task: check if ANY of its file paths are already in SEEN_FILES
110. - If NO overlap: add task to CONFLICT_FREE set, add its files to SEEN_FILES
111. - If overlap found: discard this task (keep first occurrence); log discarded task_id
112. - CONFLICT_FREE = ordered list of tasks with no file overlaps (up to BUILDER_SLOTS)
113. 
114. TRD-025-4. Launch concurrent Task() delegations for CONFLICT_FREE task set:
115. Phase A — Pre-flight (sequential, per task):
116. For each task in CONFLICT_FREE: run steps 4a-4e (architecture review, skip decision,
117. sibling context, builder selection, validate_transition) to prepare builder prompt.
118. Phase B — Concurrent dispatch:
119. Issue all Task() calls (step 4f) for CONFLICT_FREE simultaneously (parallel invocation).
120. Each builder works independently on its assigned task.
121. Phase C — Post-dispatch bookkeeping (sequential, per task):
122. For each dispatched task: run step 4g (active_builders[bead_id] = builder).
123. 
124. TRD-025-5. Wait for all parallel builders to complete:
125. - Block until ALL concurrent Task() delegations from TRD-025-4 return results
126. - Collect structured builder summaries (files_changed, implementation_description, etc.)
127. 
128. TRD-025-6. Process each builder result sequentially (after all parallel builders finish):
129. - For each completed builder result in CONFLICT_FREE order:
130. a. On builder success: run Reviewer Delegation step (step 3a) for this task
131. b. On reviewer approval: run QA Delegation step (step 3b) for this task
132. c. On any rejection: run Rejection Loop (step 3c) for this task
133. (Review and QA remain sequential per task even when builders ran in parallel)
134. - Remove each completed task from active_builders after its full pipeline finishes
135. 
136. --- Sequential Commit Ordering (TRD-026) ---
137. When parallel builders complete and their full pipelines (review+QA) pass, the lead serializes git commit operations:
138. 
139. TRD-026-1. Maintain COMMIT_QUEUE = ordered list of tasks whose pipelines completed successfully (in completion order).
140. 
141. TRD-026-2. For each task in COMMIT_QUEUE (process one at a time):
142. a. Run: git diff --name-only HEAD to see current staged changes
143. b. If no file conflicts with prior commits in this batch: commit immediately
144. - git commit with message referencing task ID and bead ID
145. - br comment add <bead_id> 'commit-order:<N> commit:<sha>'
146. - N = position in COMMIT_QUEUE (1-indexed)
147. c. If file conflict detected (same file modified by prior commit):
148. - One retry: builder re-applies its changes on top of the current HEAD
149. - If retry succeeds: commit normally, record commit-order:<N>
150. - If retry fails: br update <bead_id> --status=open; br comment add <bead_id> 'status:open commit-conflict-unresolved: returned to open'; continue to next task in COMMIT_QUEUE
151. 
152. TRD-026-3. After all commits: br sync --flush-only
153. 
154. --- Parallel Builder Failure Isolation (TRD-027) ---
155. If one builder fails (Task() returns error or structured failure summary) during parallel execution:
156. 
157. TRD-027-1. Reset that task: br update <bead_id> --status=open
158. 
159. TRD-027-2. Record failure (pure audit record — no state prefix): br comment add <bead_id> 'verdict:failed builder:<agent_type> reason:<error_summary>'
160. Note: NO status: prefix — this is a failure audit comment, not a state transition. validate_transition is NOT called.
161. The bead is already open (reset in step 1); a status: prefix would mislead get_sub_state() on the next loop iteration.
162. 
163. TRD-027-3. Other parallel builders continue unaffected (do not interrupt them).
164. 
165. TRD-027-4. The failed task enters the debug loop (Execute step 5: Debug Loop) on the NEXT loop iteration OR is eligible for re-assignment to another builder.
166. 
167. TRD-027-5. Lead tracks builder slot count: when a builder fails, its slot is freed immediately (active_builders count decremented).
168. 
169. TRD-027-6. Lead refills available slots on the next loop iteration by querying bv --robot-next / br ready again.
170. 
171. 5. Check loop exit:
172. - If no tasks returned by bv/br AND no active_builders AND no in-flight tasks: break to Completion
173. - If no tasks returned but tasks exist in_review or in_qa: wait (check in-flight tasks next iteration)
174. 
175. 6. br sync --flush-only
176. 7. Continue LOOP
177. LOOP:
178. Run: br sync --flush-only (ensure JSONL current before bv call)
179. If EXECUTE_ONLY=true: run bv --robot-next --format toon to get single top-priority task (bv is guaranteed available)
180. If EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND BV_AVAILABLE: run bv --robot-next --format toon to get single top-priority task
181. If EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND not BV_AVAILABLE: run br ready, filter by title prefix [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:task:]
182. If no tasks returned: run br list --status=open --json filtered by TRD slug; if no open tasks remain break to Completion; else PAUSE (possible dependency cycle)
183. If max_parallel==1 or single task ready: execute_single_task
184. Else if EXECUTE_ONLY=true: use bv --robot-plan --format toon for parallel tracks; take up to max_parallel candidates; run file conflict detection; execute conflict-free group in parallel
185. Else if EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND BV_AVAILABLE: use bv --robot-plan --format toon for parallel tracks; take up to max_parallel candidates; run file conflict detection; execute conflict-free group in parallel
186. Else if EXECUTE_ONLY=false AND not BV_AVAILABLE: run br ready, filter by TRD slug, take up to max_parallel candidates; run file conflict detection; execute conflict-free group in parallel
187. After each task (or parallel group): br sync --flush-only, then if EXECUTE_ONLY=true or BV_AVAILABLE run bv --robot-triage --format toon for progress check else run br list --status=open filtered by TRD slug

### Step 2: Task Claim and Specialist Selection

Claim task in beads before delegating to specialist agent

**Actions:**
1. Run: br update <BEAD_ID> --status=in_progress — skip task if exit code != 0 (already claimed)
2. Extract TASK_ID from task.title prefix (TRD-XXX pattern)
3. Select specialist by keyword matching: architecture/design/system/multi-component/cross-cutting/orchestrat -> @tech-lead-orchestrator; backend/api/endpoint/database/server/model/migration -> @backend-developer; frontend/ui/component/react/vue/angular/svelte/css -> @frontend-developer; test/spec/e2e/playwright/coverage -> @test-runner or @playwright-tester; docs/readme/documentation/changelog/api-docs -> @documentation-specialist; infra/deploy/docker/k8s/kubernetes/aws/cloud/terraform -> @infrastructure-developer; refactor/optimize/cleanup spanning multiple domains -> @tech-lead-orchestrator; default -> @backend-developer
4. Check .claude/router-rules.json first; project-specific agents take priority over keyword defaults
5. Match skills via router-rules.json triggers/patterns; fallback: jest/pytest/rspec/exunit/xunit by language keywords
6. 
7. TRD-014 — Builder Agent Constraint from Team Config (AC: FR-TD-5):
8. When TEAM_MODE=true, add this constraint to specialist selection:
9. - Run standard keyword matching (existing logic above)
10. - But constrain candidates to TEAM_ROLES.builder.agents list only
11. - If keyword match selects agent NOT in team builder list: fall back to first agent in TEAM_ROLES.builder.agents
12. - .claude/router-rules.json takes priority over both keyword defaults AND team constraint
13. - Example: keyword says @infrastructure-developer, team builder list has [backend-developer, frontend-developer]
14. -> fall back to backend-developer (first in list)
15. When TEAM_MODE=false: existing specialist selection unchanged

### Step 3: Task Delegation

Build prompt and delegate to selected specialist, require closing summary comment

**Actions:**
1. Build prompt with: Task ID + bead ID, TRD file path, strategy, constitution targets, completed tasks this phase, acceptance criteria, inferred file paths, matched skills, strategy-specific instructions
2. Builder prompt construction MUST follow these context curation rules:
3. - Include ONLY: task description from bead, TRD section for this task, architecture guidance (if any), sibling context (if any from TRD-022)
4. - Do NOT include: full TRD content, other phase tasks, conversation history, previous builder outputs
5. - Include the file path(s) the task targets so the builder can Read them directly
6. - Include the test file path(s) the task should create/modify
7. - Each builder subagent starts with clean context — this is intentional for quality
8. Include in prompt: 'When done, provide a structured summary: files changed, what was implemented, any issues encountered, and recommendations for follow-up work.'
9. Delegate: Task(agent_type=<specialist>, prompt=<prompt>)
10. On success: br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Implementation complete: <agent_summary_of_work_done — files changed, what was implemented, any issues or recommendations>'; proceed to Code Review step
11. On failure: br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Failed: <error_summary>. Files touched: <changed_files>. Agent: <specialist_type>.'; br update <BEAD_ID> --status=open; br sync --flush-only; enter debug loop
12. 
13. TRD-015 — Builder Delegation with Structured Output (AC: FR-BA-1, FR-BA-2, FR-BA-3, FR-BA-4, FR-BA-5, AC-SM-1):
14. When TEAM_MODE=true, builder delegation differs:
15. 0. Builder prompt construction MUST follow these context curation rules:
16. - Include ONLY: task description from bead, TRD section for this task, architecture guidance (if any), sibling context (if any from TRD-022)
17. - Do NOT include: full TRD content, other phase tasks, conversation history, previous builder outputs
18. - Include the file path(s) the task targets so the builder can Read them directly
19. - Include the test file path(s) the task should create/modify
20. - Each builder subagent starts with clean context — this is intentional for quality
21. 1. Builder prompt includes additional instruction:
22. 'IMPORTANT: Do NOT close this bead (do not run br close). When done, return a structured summary:'
23. '  - files_changed: [list of modified files with paths]'
24. '  - implementation_description: [what was implemented]'
25. '  - test_results: [pass/fail with details]'
26. '  - issues_encountered: [list of problems, empty if none]'
27. '  - recommendations: [follow-up suggestions, empty if none]'
28. 2. Delegate: Task(subagent_type=<builder>, prompt=<augmented_prompt>)
29. 3. On success:
30. a. Extract files_changed from builder's structured summary
31. b. Run: br comment add <bead_id> 'status:in_review builder:<agent> files:<comma-joined-files_changed>'
32. c. validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_review') -- see Execute step 9 (State Machine Transition Validator)
33. d. Proceed to Reviewer Delegation (TRD-016, Execute step 3a)
34. 4. On failure (builder crashes, not rejection):
35. a. br comment add <bead_id> 'Implementation failed: <error_summary>. Builder: <agent>.'
36. b. Enter debug loop (Execute step 5)
37. When TEAM_MODE=false: existing Task Delegation step unchanged (builder can close bead)

### Step 4: Reviewer Delegation and Verdict Handling (TEAM_MODE=true only)

Delegate to reviewer after builder submits, parse verdict, route accordingly. AC: FR-CR-1, FR-CR-2, FR-CR-3, FR-CR-4, FR-CR-5, FR-CR-6, FR-LL-3, FR-SM-5, AC-SM-3

**Actions:**
1. TRD-016 — Reviewer Delegation and Verdict Handling (TEAM_MODE=true only, after builder writes status:in_review comment):
2. 
3. 1. Build reviewer prompt:
4. - Bead ID and task title
5. - Files changed (from builder's structured summary)
6. - Builder's implementation_description
7. - TRD acceptance criteria for this specific task (from bead description)
8. - Strategy and coverage targets
9. - Relevant test results from builder summary
10. - Instruction: 'Review the implementation. Return verdict: APPROVED or REJECTED.'
11. 'If REJECTED: provide specific feedback with file, line, issue, and suggestion.'
12. 
13. 2. Delegate: Task(subagent_type=<TEAM_ROLES.reviewer.agents[0]>, prompt=<reviewer_prompt>)
14. 
15. 3. Parse reviewer response:
16. - Look for 'APPROVED' or 'REJECTED' keyword in response
17. - Extract rejection reason if REJECTED
18. 
19. 4. On APPROVED:
20. - validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_qa')  -- sole status comment writer: writes 'status:in_qa reviewer:<agent> verdict:approved' and calls br comment add internally
21. - Proceed to QA Delegation (TRD-017)
22. 
23. 5. On REJECTED:
24. - validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_progress')  -- sole status comment writer: writes 'status:in_progress reviewer:<agent> verdict:rejected reason:<url_encoded_reason>' and resets native br status to open
25. - Increment rejection count for this bead
26. - Re-delegate to builder with rejection context (TRD-018 rejection loop)
27. 
28. 6. Track: record reviewer_agent for this task in metrics (TRD-034)

### Step 5: QA Delegation and Verdict Handling (TEAM_MODE=true only)

Delegate to QA agent after reviewer approves and task is in_qa state, parse verdict, route accordingly. AC: FR-QA-1, FR-QA-2, FR-QA-3, FR-QA-4, FR-QA-5, FR-QA-6, FR-LL-4, FR-SM-6, AC-SM-1

**Actions:**
1. TRD-017 — QA Delegation and Verdict Handling (TEAM_MODE=true only, invoked when task sub-state == 'in_qa'):
2. 
3. 1. Build QA prompt:
4. - Bead ID and task title
5. - Files changed (from builder's structured summary)
6. - Builder's implementation_description
7. - Reviewer's verdict (approved) and any notes from reviewer response
8. - TRD acceptance criteria for this specific task (from bead description)
9. - Strategy and coverage targets (from strategy detection)
10. - Test results from builder's summary
11. 
12. 2. Delegate to @test-runner first for fresh test execution:
13. Task(subagent_type='test-runner', prompt='Run tests for changed files: <files_changed>. Report pass/fail and coverage.')
14. Capture test_results from @test-runner response.
15. 
16. 3. Build augmented QA prompt with test_results, then:
17. Before accessing TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID]: if TASK_ID is not present as a key in the TASK_TRACEABILITY map (task predates traceability or map rebuild failed): print 'WARNING: No traceability entry for <TASK_ID> — treating as impl task; audit tokens will not be written. If this task has [satisfies REQ-NNN] annotations, re-run this command to re-scaffold.' Set is_test_task = false and skip all req-satisfied/req-verified/ac-proven token writing for this task.
18. Check TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].is_test_task:
19. If is_test_task == true: append to QA prompt before delegating:
20. 'IMPORTANT: For each PRD AC listed in Validates PRD ACs:, provide explicit verdict (PROVEN/NOT_PROVEN) with evidence.'
21. Delegate: Task(subagent_type=<TEAM_ROLES.qa.agents[0]>, prompt=<qa_prompt_with_test_results>)
22. 
23. QA agent validates:
24. - All acceptance criteria from TRD are met
25. - Test coverage meets strategy targets
26. - No regressions in existing tests
27. - Code quality and security concerns (if any)
28. 
29. QA returns verdict: PASSED or REJECTED with specific issues.
30. 
31. 4. Parse QA response for PASSED/REJECTED keyword.
32. 
33. 5. On PASSED:
34. a. Detect if this is a test task: apply TASK_TRACEABILITY absent-key guard (see Execute step 3b, action item 3). Otherwise: check TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].is_test_task
35. b. If test task: parse QA response for per-AC verdicts (the prompt already required PROVEN/NOT_PROVEN per AC from step 3 above);
36. build PROVEN_ACS list of AC-NNN-M IDs with verdict=PROVEN
37. Extract REQ_ID from TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].satisfies_req_id
38. c. validate_transition(bead_id, 'closed') — writes augmented status comment for test tasks:
39. If test task: 'status:closed qa:<agent> verdict:passed req-satisfied:<REQ_ID> ac-proven:<PROVEN_ACS comma-joined>'
40. If impl task: 'status:closed qa:<agent> verdict:passed' (existing behavior)
41. d. If test task AND PASSED: write root epic audit comment:
42. br comment add <ROOT_EPIC_ID> 'req-verified:<REQ_ID> by:<TASK_ID> qa:<QA_AGENT> ac-proven:<PROVEN_ACS comma-joined>'
43. e. Run: br sync --flush-only
44. f. Update TRD checkbox: replace '- [ ] **<TASK_ID>**' with '- [x] **<TASK_ID>**' in TRD file
45. g. git commit -m 'feat(<TRD_SLUG>): complete <TASK_ID> - <task_title_short>'
46. h. Record metrics: update TEAM_METRICS accumulator (TRD-034, Execute step 3d)
47. 
48. 6. On REJECTED:
49. a. validate_transition(bead_id, 'in_progress')  -- sole status comment writer: writes 'status:in_progress qa:<agent> verdict:rejected reason:<url_encoded_reason>' and resets native br status to open
50. b. Run: br update <bead_id> --status=open (ensure br native status is reset for re-claiming)
51. c. Increment rejection count for this bead
52. d. Return to builder with QA feedback (via rejection loop TRD-018, Execute step 3c):
53. - Include: QA rejection reason, failed acceptance criteria, test failure details
54. - Builder re-implements, then goes back through reviewer -> QA
55. 
56. 7. Track: record qa_agent for this task in metrics (TRD-034)

### Step 6: Rejection Loop with Builder Re-Delegation (TEAM_MODE=true only)

Triggered by reviewer REJECTED (from step 3a) or QA REJECTED (from step 3b). Collects full rejection context, enforces MAX_REJECTIONS cap, and re-delegates to the original builder or escalates to lead. AC: FR-LL-5, AC-SM-3, AC-SM-4

**Actions:**
1. TRD-018 — Rejection Loop with Builder Re-Delegation (TEAM_MODE=true, triggered by reviewer OR QA REJECTED verdict):
2. 
3. 1. Collect full rejection context from br comments:
4. - Run: br comment list <bead_id>
5. - Extract ALL verdict:rejected comments (not just the latest)
6. - Build rejection_context = {
7. rejection_count: <count of lines containing verdict:rejected>,
8. latest_reason: <url_decoded reason from most recent verdict:rejected comment>,
9. previous_attempts: <summaries of builder implementation from prior builder comments>,
10. reviewer_feedback: <specific reviewer issues if rejection source is reviewer (step 3a)>,
11. qa_feedback: <specific QA issues including failed ACs and test failures if rejection source is QA (step 3b)>
12. }
13. 
14. 2. Check rejection_count against MAX_REJECTIONS (default 2):
15. a. If rejection_count < MAX_REJECTIONS:
16. - Identify original builder from the bead's assigned: metadata in the first status:in_progress comment
17. (Run: br comment list <bead_id>, scan forward for first 'status:in_progress assigned:<agent>' comment)
18. - Re-delegate to SAME original builder:
19. Task(subagent_type=<original_builder>, prompt=<augmented_re_delegation_prompt>)
20. - Augmented re-delegation prompt includes:
21. 'IMPORTANT: Your previous implementation was rejected. You must address ALL issues before resubmitting.'
22. 'Rejection reason: <latest_reason>'
23. 'Reviewer feedback: <reviewer_feedback if rejection from reviewer, else N/A>'
24. 'QA feedback: <qa_feedback if rejection from QA, else N/A>'
25. 'Previous attempt summary: <what was tried in prior implementation>'
26. 'Please fix ALL identified issues. Do NOT close the bead — return a structured summary as before.'
27. - After builder returns: continue through full pipeline (step 3a reviewer -> step 3b QA again)
28. 
29. b. If rejection_count >= MAX_REJECTIONS:
30. - LEAD ARCHITECTURAL REVIEW (TRD-007 escalation):
31. Lead reviews all of: task description (from bead description), all rejection reasons
32. (from br comment list), builder implementation attempts (from builder status comments),
33. and acceptance criteria (from bead description).
34. Lead decision options (choose one):
35. - Restructure task: break into smaller sub-tasks, create new child beads under same story
36. - Adjust acceptance criteria: if criteria are overly strict or misspecified for scope
37. - Reassign: delegate to a different builder from TEAM_ROLES.builder.agents list
38. - Architectural fix: identify root architectural issue requiring a fundamentally different approach
39. Record lead decision:
40. br comment add <bead_id> 'lead-escalation:max-rejections lead:tech-lead-orchestrator action:<decision>'
41. Reset rejection baseline:
42. Write a new 'status:in_progress assigned:<builder> lead-reset:true' comment.
43. Subsequent rejection counts are relative to comments AFTER this new baseline comment.
44. Allow one more full cycle (builder -> reviewer -> QA) with lead guidance included in builder prompt:
45. 'Lead architectural guidance after escalation: <lead_decision_and_rationale>'
46. If still failing after the post-escalation cycle:
47. PAUSE for user decision (abort task, force-close with known issues, or escalate further)
48. 
49. 3. All rejection history from br comments is included in re-delegation prompt.
50. Agents have full audit trail context from previous cycles via br comment list output.

### Step 7: Team Metrics Collection (TEAM_MODE=true only)

In-memory metrics accumulator invoked after each task closure in step 3b PASSED path. Tracks per-builder stats, rejection cycles, and time-in-state for the current phase. AC: FR-TM-1, FR-TM-2, FR-TM-3, AC-TM-4

**Actions:**
1. TRD-034 — Team Metrics Collection (TEAM_MODE=true, invoked after each task closure in step 3b PASSED path):
2. 
3. Initialization (once at Execute phase start when TEAM_MODE=true):
4. TEAM_METRICS = {
5. phase: <current_phase_number>,
6. tasks_completed: 0,
7. builders: {},   # per-builder stats: {agent_name: {tasks, first_pass_approvals, rejections}}
8. task_details: []  # per-task metrics entries
9. }
10. 
11. After each task closure (step 3b PASSED path):
12. 
13. 1. Identify builder agent:
14. - Run: br comment list <bead_id>
15. - Scan forward for the first (or most recent post-lead-reset) 'status:in_progress assigned:<agent>' comment
16. - Extract builder_agent name from the assigned: value
17. 
18. 2. Count rejection cycles:
19. - From the same br comment list output, count lines containing the exact token 'verdict:rejected'
20. - rejection_cycles = count of such lines
21. 
22. 3. Extract timestamps from br comments to calculate time in each sub-state:
23. - Scan br comment list output for status transition comments with timestamps
24. - time_in_progress: timestamp(first status:in_review comment) - timestamp(status:in_progress comment)
25. - time_in_review:   timestamp(status:in_qa comment) - timestamp(status:in_review comment)
26. - time_in_qa:       timestamp(status:closed comment) - timestamp(status:in_qa comment)
27. - If a stage was skipped (status:skip-review or status:skip-qa present): time for that stage = 0
28. - If timestamps are unavailable or unparse-able: record as null (do not fail metrics collection)
29. 
30. 4. Determine first_pass_approval:
31. - first_pass_approval = true if rejection_cycles == 0, else false
32. 
33. 5. Update TEAM_METRICS:
34. - TEAM_METRICS.tasks_completed += 1
35. - If builder_agent NOT in TEAM_METRICS.builders: initialize entry
36. TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent] = {tasks: 0, first_pass_approvals: 0, rejections: 0}
37. - TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].tasks += 1
38. - If first_pass_approval: TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].first_pass_approvals += 1
39. - TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].rejections += rejection_cycles
40. - TEAM_METRICS.task_details.append({
41. id: <bead_id>,
42. task_id: <TRD-XXX from title>,
43. builder: builder_agent,
44. rejection_cycles: rejection_cycles,
45. time_in_progress: time_in_progress,
46. time_in_review: time_in_review,
47. time_in_qa: time_in_qa,
48. first_pass_approval: first_pass_approval
49. })
50. 
51. Note: TEAM_METRICS lives in-memory for the duration of the Execute phase.
52. At Quality Gate phase completion (step 3 of Quality Gate), include TEAM_METRICS summary
53. in the gate result comment if TEAM_MODE=true:
54. br comment add <STORY_BEAD_ID> 'team-metrics phase:<N> tasks:<tasks_completed>
55. first-pass-rate:<X%> total-rejections:<Y>'

### Step 8: Code Review

Mandatory code review before task closure — delegate to @code-reviewer for quality validation

**Actions:**
1. Delegate to @code-reviewer: 'Review the changes for task <TASK_ID> (bead: <BEAD_ID>). Files changed: <changed_files>. Strategy: <strategy>. Check for: correctness, adherence to project conventions, security issues, test coverage, and code quality. Provide: approval/rejection with specific feedback.'
2. If approved:
3. br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Code review PASSED by @code-reviewer: <review_summary>'
4. Apply TASK_TRACEABILITY absent-key guard (see Execute step 3b, first instance in step 3).
5. Check TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].is_test_task:
6. If is_test_task == true:
7. Extract REQ_ID from TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].satisfies_req_id
8. Extract PROVEN_ACS from TASK_TRACEABILITY[TASK_ID].validates_acs (all ACs for this test task)
9. Write the status:closed comment with embedded req-satisfied token: run `br close <BEAD_ID>` then write bead comment: `br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'status:closed reviewer:code-reviewer verdict:approved req-satisfied:<REQ_ID> ac-proven:<PROVEN_ACS comma-joined>'`
10. Write root epic comment: br comment add <ROOT_EPIC_ID> 'req-verified:<REQ_ID> by:<TASK_ID> reviewer:code-reviewer ac-proven:<PROVEN_ACS comma-joined>'
11. Note: In TEAM_MODE=false, code review approval triggers audit token writing. The 'reviewer:code-reviewer' field in the comment distinguishes this from QA-verified evidence. The Completion Report will show these as SATISFIED(code-review) to distinguish from SATISFIED(qa-verified).
12. br sync --flush-only; update TRD checkbox - [ ] -> - [x]; git commit
13. If is_test_task == false:
14. br close <BEAD_ID> --reason='Completed — code review passed'; br sync --flush-only; update TRD checkbox - [ ] -> - [x]; git commit
15. If rejected with fixable issues: br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Code review REJECTED: <issues_found>'; delegate back to original specialist with review feedback; re-submit to code review after fixes (max 2 review rounds)
16. If rejected after 2 rounds: br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Code review failed after 2 rounds. Issues: <remaining_issues>.'; PAUSE for user decision (force-close, fix manually, abort)
17. Skip code review only if strategy == 'flexible' or task type is docs/documentation-only

### Step 9: Debug Loop

Attempt automated fix on task failure via deep-debugger (max 2 retries)

**Actions:**
1. Delegate to @deep-debugger with error details, changed files, strategy, bead ID
2. If fix applied: re-run tests; if pass -> proceed to Code Review step (order 4); if fail -> retry
3. After 2 retries: br comment add <BEAD_ID> 'Debug loop exhausted after 2 retries. Root cause: <error_analysis>. Attempted fixes: <fix_attempts>. Manual intervention required.'; br sync --flush-only; PAUSE for user decision
4. 
5. TRD-019 — Debug Loop Integration for Team Mode (AC: FR-IT-5, AC-LL-2):
6. When TEAM_MODE=true and builder fails with an actual error (not rejection -- crash/exception):
7. 1. Enter debug loop same as v2.1.0 (delegate to @deep-debugger with error details, changed files)
8. 2. Record debug attempt: br comment add <bead_id> 'debug-attempt:<N> debugger:deep-debugger error:<summary>'
9. 3. If fix applied: re-run through builder (Task(builder, re-implementation prompt))
10. Then continue through handoff pipeline: reviewer -> QA
11. 4. After 2 debug retries without success:
12. br comment add <bead_id> 'Debug loop exhausted after 2 retries. Manual intervention required.'
13. PAUSE for user decision (same as v2.1.0)
14. When TEAM_MODE=false: existing debug loop unchanged

### Step 10: Error Handling

Handle br command failures during execution

**Actions:**
1. After any br command: if exit code != 0 AND prior br commands in session succeeded -> possible br failure
2. Print error message with br command that failed and its exit code
3. Print: check br status and .beads/ directory integrity
4. PAUSE for user decision (resume with /ensemble:implement-trd-beads <trd-path> after issue resolved)

### Step 11: Utility: Sub-State Query Function (get_sub_state)

Inline utility referenced by the State Machine Transition Validator (order 8) and by resume logic. Reads br comment history in reverse to find the most recent status: comment. AC: FR-SM-2, FR-BR-2, AC-BR-2

**Actions:**
1. Function signature: get_sub_state(bead_id) -> (state, metadata_dict)
2. Step 1: Run: br comment list <bead_id>  — capture full output as COMMENT_LIST
3. Step 2: Split COMMENT_LIST into individual lines
4. Step 3: Scan lines in REVERSE ORDER (last line first; last comment is most recent)
5. Step 4: For each line, check if the line STARTS WITH the exact prefix 'status:' (not merely contains it)
6. Step 5 — If a matching line is found:
7. a. Extract state: first whitespace-delimited token after 'status:' (e.g., 'in_progress', 'in_review', 'in_qa', 'closed')
8. b. Extract metadata: remaining space-separated 'key:value' tokens on the same line
9. c. URL-decode any 'reason:' values: replace '%20' with space and '+' with space
10. d. Return (state, {key: value, ...})
11. Step 6 — Edge cases during line scan:
12. - Malformed comment where 'status:' prefix is present but no state token follows: skip that line and continue scanning
13. - Multiple rapid status comments: reverse scan naturally returns the most recent — correct behavior
14. - Empty comment list: falls through to Step 7 (native status lookup)
15. Step 7 — If NO 'status:' comment found in entire list (fallback to br native status):
16. Run: br list --json, filter JSON array for the entry where .id == bead_id, read .status field
17. Map native status to sub-state:
18. 'open'        -> return ('open', {})
19. 'in_progress' -> return ('in_progress', {})
20. 'closed'      -> return ('closed', {})
21. any other     -> return (native_status_value, {})

### Step 12: Utility: Rejection Cycle Tracking and Cap

Inline utility invoked after any verdict:rejected comment is written during reviewer or QA delegation. Enforces a maximum rejection cap and escalates to lead when the cap is reached. AC: FR-SM-7, AC-SM-4

**Actions:**
1. Rejection Cycle Tracking (invoked during reviewer and QA delegation, after each verdict:rejected comment):
2. Step 1 — Count rejection cycles for this bead (respecting lead-reset baseline):
3. Run: br comment list <bead_id>  — capture full output as COMMENT_LINES (ordered oldest to newest)
4. 1a. Scan all of COMMENT_LINES (oldest to newest, index 0 upward), updating BASELINE_LINE_INDEX each time a line containing 'lead-reset:true' is encountered.
5. After the full scan: BASELINE_LINE_INDEX holds the index of the MOST RECENT such line.
6. If no such line was found: BASELINE_LINE_INDEX = -1 (no baseline; count all comments).
7. 1b. Count lines containing the exact token 'verdict:rejected' in COMMENT_LINES[BASELINE_LINE_INDEX+1:]
8. (i.e., only comments appearing AFTER the baseline comment, or all comments if no baseline).
9. REJECTION_COUNT = number of matching lines found in the post-baseline slice
10. Step 2 — Determine cap:
11. MAX_REJECTIONS = 2 (default)
12. If team config contains a max_rejections: field for this role: override MAX_REJECTIONS with that value
13. Step 3 — If REJECTION_COUNT < MAX_REJECTIONS:
14. Return task to builder with full rejection context:
15. - Include: rejection reason from verdict:rejected comment, all reviewer/QA feedback from comments, list of previous attempt summaries
16. Continue normal rejection loop (builder re-implements, re-submits)
17. Step 4 — If REJECTION_COUNT >= MAX_REJECTIONS, escalate to lead for architectural review:
18. a. Lead reviews: task description, all rejection reasons (from br comment list), builder implementation attempts (from file changes), acceptance criteria
19. b. Lead may take any of these actions:
20. - Restructure the task into smaller sub-tasks
21. - Adjust acceptance criteria if determined to be overly strict
22. - Reassign to a different builder agent
23. - Identify and resolve underlying architectural issues
24. c. Record lead decision:
25. br comment add <bead_id> 'lead-escalation:max-rejections-reached lead:tech-lead-orchestrator action:<lead_decision>'
26. d. Reset tracking baseline:
27. Write a new 'status:in_progress' comment from lead — this becomes the new baseline for REJECTION_COUNT
28. (Subsequent rejection counts are relative to comments after this new baseline)
29. e. Allow one additional review cycle with lead's guidance included in the builder prompt
30. Step 5 — If still failing after lead escalation:
31. PAUSE for user decision (abort task, force-close, or escalate further)

### Step 13: Utility: State Machine Transition Validator

Inline utility called before any status transition to verify it is legal and then execute it atomically. References get_sub_state (order 7). AC: FR-SM-1, FR-SM-4, FR-SM-8, AC-SM-5

**Actions:**
1. Valid transitions table (current_state -> [allowed target_states]):
2. open        -> in_progress   (actor: lead)
3. in_progress -> in_review     (actor: builder)
4. in_progress -> in_qa         (actor: lead, when REVIEWER_ENABLED=false)
5. in_progress -> closed        (actor: lead, when REVIEWER_ENABLED=false AND QA_ENABLED=false)
6. in_review   -> in_qa         (actor: reviewer, verdict: approved)
7. in_review   -> in_progress   (actor: reviewer, verdict: rejected)
8. in_qa       -> closed        (actor: qa, verdict: passed)
9. in_qa       -> in_progress   (actor: qa, verdict: rejected)
10. Validation and transition algorithm (call this before any status change):
11. Step 1: call get_sub_state(bead_id) — capture (current_state, metadata)
12. Step 2: look up VALID_TRANSITIONS[current_state] to get the set of allowed target states
13. Step 3: if target_state NOT in allowed set:
14. Print: 'ERROR: Invalid transition from {current_state} to {target_state} for bead {bead_id}'
15. HALT
16. Step 4: if target_state IS valid, execute the transition:
17. a. Build comment_string: 'status:{target_state} {key}:{value} ...' (include actor, verdict, and any other relevant metadata keys from caller)
18. b. Run: br comment add <bead_id> '<comment_string>'
19. c. Run: br sync --flush-only
20. d. If target_state == 'closed': run br close <bead_id> --reason='<metadata.reason if provided, else QA passed>'
21. e. If target_state == 'in_progress' AND current_state was 'in_review' or 'in_qa' (i.e., a rejection path):
22. Run: br update <bead_id> --status=open  (reset native br status so lead can re-assign)

## Phase 4: Quality Gate

### Step 1: Phase Completion Detection

Detect when all tasks in a phase are closed

**Actions:**
1. After each task completion: run br list --status=open --json, filter by title prefix [trd:<TRD_SLUG>:phase:<N>] to find tasks for this phase
2. If no open tasks remain for this phase: trigger quality gate for this story/phase

### Step 2: Test Execution

Delegate test suite execution to test-runner, with scope adjusted for team mode. AC: FR-QA-7

**Actions:**
1. TRD-031 — Phase Quality Gate Scope Reduction for Team QA (AC: FR-QA-7):
2. 
3. When TEAM_MODE=true AND QA_ENABLED=true (per-task QA already ran via step 3b for each task):
4. Phase gate focuses on INTEGRATION-ONLY scope:
5. a. Delegate to @test-runner: run INTEGRATION test suite only for files modified in this phase
6. (exclude unit tests — these were already validated per-task by QA agent)
7. Prompt: 'Run integration test suite for phase <N> files: <files_list>. Report pass/fail and integration coverage %.'
8. b. Cross-file consistency checks:
9. - Verify API contracts are consistent across changed files (no mismatched request/response shapes)
10. - Verify shared types and interfaces are used consistently across module boundaries
11. - Verify module import/export contracts are intact (no broken references)
12. c. Aggregate coverage report:
13. - Collect per-task coverage data from QA verdict comments (verdict:passed lines with coverage metadata)
14. - Compute aggregate coverage across all phase tasks
15. - Confirm aggregate coverage meets strategy target
16. Parse results: gate_passed = integration_tests_pass AND aggregate_coverage >= target
17. Exception: strategy=characterization or flexible -> gate_passed = true (informational only)
18. Note: Phase gate does NOT re-run per-task unit tests or per-task AC validation (already done by QA per task).
19. 
20. When TEAM_MODE=false (v2.1.0 single-agent mode — no per-task QA ran, full gate required):
21. Delegate to @test-runner: run full test suite (unit + integration) for files modified in this phase;
22. report pass/fail, unit coverage %, integration coverage %, failures with file:line
23. Parse results: gate_passed = tests_pass AND unit_cov >= target AND int_cov >= target
24. Exception: strategy=characterization or flexible -> gate_passed = true (informational only)
25. 
26. When TEAM_MODE=true AND QA_ENABLED=false (team mode active but no QA agent configured — no per-task QA ran, full gate required):
27. Delegate to @test-runner: run full test suite (unit + integration) for files modified in this phase;
28. report pass/fail, unit coverage %, integration coverage %, failures with file:line
29. Parse results: gate_passed = tests_pass AND unit_cov >= target AND int_cov >= target
30. Exception: strategy=characterization or flexible -> gate_passed = true (informational only)

### Step 3: Gate Result Recording

Record quality gate outcome as br comment and close story on pass

**Actions:**
1. TRD-031 — Gate result comment format depends on scope (team mode vs full):
2. 
3. When TEAM_MODE=true AND QA_ENABLED=true (integration-only scope):
4. Run: br comment add <STORY_BEAD_ID> 'Quality gate result: <PASS|FAIL> | integration: <X tests> | aggregate-coverage: <Y%> | scope: integration-only (team-QA)'
5. If TEAM_METRICS is populated (TRD-034): append team metrics summary:
6. br comment add <STORY_BEAD_ID> 'team-metrics phase:<N> tasks:<tasks_completed> first-pass-rate:<X%> total-rejections:<Y>'
7. 
8. When TEAM_MODE=false (v2.1.0 single-agent mode — no per-task QA ran, full unit + integration gate required):
9. Run: br comment add <STORY_BEAD_ID> 'Quality gate result: <PASS|FAIL> | unit: <X%> | integration: <Y%> | strategy: <strategy>'
10. 
11. When TEAM_MODE=true AND QA_ENABLED=false (team mode but no QA agent configured — no per-task QA ran, full unit + integration gate required):
12. Run: br comment add <STORY_BEAD_ID> 'Quality gate result: <PASS|FAIL> | unit: <X%> | integration: <Y%> | strategy: <strategy>'
13. 
14. Run: br sync --flush-only
15. If gate_passed: br close <STORY_BEAD_ID> --reason='Phase complete - quality gate passed'; br sync --flush-only; git commit -m 'chore(phase <N>): checkpoint (tests pass; unit <X%>, int <Y%>)'
16. If gate_passed AND more phases remain AND TEAM_MODE=true: reset TEAM_METRICS for the next phase:
17. TEAM_METRICS = { phase: <N+1>, tasks_completed: 0, builders: {}, task_details: [] }
18. (This ensures phase N+1 accumulates fresh metrics and does not inherit stale phase-N data.)
19. If NOT gate_passed AND blocking strategy (tdd/refactor/bug-fix): print gate failure details; PAUSE for user: fix/skip/abort

### Step 4: Team Performance Summary (TEAM_MODE=true only)

Print and persist team performance metrics after Quality Gate step 3 when TEAM_MODE=true and TEAM_METRICS is populated. AC: FR-TM-4, FR-TM-5

**Actions:**
1. TRD-035 — Team Performance Summary (TEAM_MODE=true AND TEAM_METRICS populated, after Gate Result Recording):
2. 
3. Condition: execute this step only when TEAM_MODE=true AND TEAM_METRICS.tasks_completed > 0
4. 
5. 1. Compute derived metrics:
6. - total_rejections = sum of rejection_cycles across all entries in TEAM_METRICS.task_details
7. - first_pass_rate = (first_pass_approvals_count / TEAM_METRICS.tasks_completed) * 100
8. where first_pass_approvals_count = number of entries in TEAM_METRICS.task_details where first_pass_approval == true
9. - Per-builder breakdown: for each builder_agent in TEAM_METRICS.builders:
10. builder_tasks = TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].tasks
11. builder_first_pass = TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].first_pass_approvals
12. builder_rejections = TEAM_METRICS.builders[builder_agent].rejections
13. 
14. 2. Print team performance summary block to console:
15. === TEAM PERFORMANCE SUMMARY — Phase <TEAM_METRICS.phase> ===
16. Tasks completed: <TEAM_METRICS.tasks_completed>
17. First-pass approval rate: <first_pass_rate>%
18. Total rejection cycles: <total_rejections>
19. Per-builder breakdown:
20. <builder_agent>: tasks=<builder_tasks> first-pass=<builder_first_pass> rejections=<builder_rejections>
21. (repeat for each builder in TEAM_METRICS.builders)
22. === END TEAM SUMMARY ===
23. 
24. 3. Persist metrics as br comment on ROOT_EPIC_ID (not story bead — epic captures cross-phase history):
25. - Build BUILDERS_JSON per TRD-036 schema (see below)
26. - Run: br comment add <ROOT_EPIC_ID> 'team-metrics:phase-<N> tasks:<tasks_completed> first-pass-rate:<first_pass_rate>% total-rejections:<total_rejections> builders:<BUILDERS_JSON>'
27. 
28. TRD-036 — BUILDERS_JSON Schema:
29. BUILDERS_JSON is a JSON-encoded object with the following top-level structure:
30. {
31. "phase": <N>,
32. "tasks_completed": <integer>,
33. "first_pass_rate": <float, 0.0-100.0>,
34. "total_rejections": <integer>,
35. "avg_rejection_cycles": <float>,
36. "builders": {
37. "<builder_agent_type>": {
38. "tasks": <integer>,
39. "first_pass_approvals": <integer>,
40. "rejections": <integer>,
41. "first_pass_rate": <float, 0.0-100.0>
42. }
43. }
44. }
45. Derivation rules:
46. - avg_rejection_cycles = total_rejections / tasks_completed (0.0 if tasks_completed == 0)
47. - Per-builder first_pass_rate = (first_pass_approvals / tasks) * 100
48. - top-level first_pass_rate = (first_pass_approvals_count / tasks_completed) * 100 (same as step 1 above)
49. 
50. 4. Run: br sync --flush-only (ensure metrics comment is persisted)

## Phase 5: Completion

### Step 1: Epic Closure

Close the root epic when all children are done

**Actions:**
1. Verify: br list --status=open --json filtered by TRD slug returns no open tasks
2. Run: br close <ROOT_EPIC_ID> --reason='TRD implementation complete'
3. Run: br sync --flush-only

### Step 2: TRD Checkbox Sync

Update TRD file checkboxes to reflect bead closure state

**Actions:**
1. For each task in TRD Master Task List: if TRD_TO_BEAD_MAP[task.id] exists and bead status == 'closed' -> replace '- [ ] **<task.id>**' with '- [x] **<task.id>**'
2. git commit -m 'docs(TRD): sync checkboxes to bead closure state'

### Step 3: Completion Report

Print final summary and remind user about PR creation

**Actions:**
1. Print completion report: TRD file, branch, strategy, epic ID, task counts, coverage summary
2. Requirement Satisfaction Table: scan ROOT_EPIC_ID comments for req-verified: tokens
3. Run: br comment list <ROOT_EPIC_ID>
4. If br comment list fails or returns non-JSON: print 'WARNING: Could not read root epic comments — req-verified data unavailable. Run /ensemble:requirement-status <TRD_SLUG> to generate the report manually.' Continue with empty VERIFIED_REQS.
5. Parse each comment for tokens: req-verified:REQ-NNN, by:TRD-NNN-TEST, qa:<agent>, reviewer:<agent>, ac-proven:AC-NNN-M,...
6. Build VERIFIED_REQS map: REQ-NNN -> {test_task, qa_agent, reviewer_agent, acs_proven, verification_mode}
7. For each req-verified token: check if the comment contains 'reviewer:code-reviewer' (TEAM_MODE=false code-review path) vs 'qa:<agent>' (TEAM_MODE=true QA path); set verification_mode accordingly: 'code-review' or 'qa-verified'
8. If TRD has PRD reference: also load PRD REQ-NNN list for cross-reference (unverified reqs show as NOT VERIFIED)
9. Print table:
10. === REQUIREMENT SATISFACTION REPORT ===
11. REQ-001: SATISFIED(qa-verified) (TRD-001-TEST) — ACs: AC-001-1, AC-001-2
12. REQ-002: NOT VERIFIED (TRD-002-TEST still open)
13. REQ-003: SATISFIED(code-review) (TRD-007-TEST) — ACs: AC-003-1, AC-003-2, AC-003-3
14. TOTAL: <N> satisfied / <M> total requirements
15. Note: SATISFIED(code-review) = verified via TEAM_MODE=false code-reviewer approval; SATISFIED(qa-verified) = verified via TEAM_MODE=true QA agent
16. ========================================
17. Run: br sync --flush-only
18. If BV_AVAILABLE: run bv --robot-triage --format toon for final progress summary
19. If not BV_AVAILABLE: run br list --status=open --json filtered by TRD slug (expect empty)
20. Remind user: git diff main...<branch>; gh pr create; after merge: mv <trd_file> docs/TRD/completed/
21. Remind user: br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/ && git commit -m 'chore: final beads sync'
22. TIP: The execution engine used here is also available standalone as /ensemble:beads-build <epic-id>. Use it to drive any bead hierarchy (not just TRD-generated ones) through the same build pipeline.
23. Do NOT auto-create PR — user must run gh pr create manually
